title: goal_id parent_goal goal_type
purpose: A1.1 defines goal id parent goal goal type for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified.
scope: Owns `A1.1` only; siblings under `A1` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §3 — Branch A — Pursuit & control plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#3-branch-a-pursuit-control-plane).
behavior_steps: At H1 plan approval, the conductor stamps goal_id, optional parent_goal, and goal_type into state.json and the journal—the identity record every pursuit wake reloads before routing or verification.

On each wake, [A2.1](A2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) reads goal_type to bind contracts: milestone goals require manifest gates, feature goals bind task cards and evidence paths, and company_ops goals follow the ops queue.
When parent_goal is set, child pursuits inherit roll-up scope so parallel program streams report status to the parent without duplicating identity at each leaf or losing H3 roll-up.

The conductor and journal-keeper dual-write any H1 amendments to these fields; [B1.1](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md) scripts refuse progression when goal_id collides or parent_goal points to a missing ancestor.

If identity drifts—duplicate ids, wrong parent—pursuit halts at H2, evidence must not attach to the wrong scope, and [A1.3](A1.3-goal-verify-command-meta-test.md) cannot pass until fields are reconciled.

Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
goal_verify passes but H3 rejected â€” goal.state returns to pursuing with rejection notes.
Edge case `A1.1` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `A1.1`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.
failure_modes: **Silent stop:** Agent ends turn without updating queue â†’ mitigated by /loop + check-hierarchy-queue.py EMPTY gate.
**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Add `goal` block to state template and journal-keeper dual-write (v2.14).
Implement `scripts/goal-verify.py` stub aggregating task evidence paths.
Document `A1.1` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `A1.1`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: A1.1 defines goal id parent goal goal type for the agent-driven expert system. Pursuit & control plane — autonomous loops until goal verified. In practice, the system at h1 plan approval, the conductor stamps goal_id, optional parent_goal, and goal_type into state.json and the journal—the identity record every pursuit wake reloads before routing or verification, then on each wake, [a2.1](a2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) reads goal_type to bind contracts: milestone goals require manifest gates, feature goals bind task cards and evidence paths, and company_ops goals follow the ops queue.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent pilot 2026-06-28 -->

Every pursuit in the expert system begins with a goal record that can be found again after restarts, nested under the right parent, and classified by type. This capability defines the three identity fields that make that possible: a unique goal_id, an optional parent_goal for tree structure, and goal_type (app, feature, milestone, or company_ops).

The type enum is not cosmetic. It tells preflight, routing, and verification which contracts apply—a milestone goal may require manifest approval while a feature goal binds to task cards and evidence paths. Nesting via parent_goal lets a program decompose into parallel streams without losing roll-up status at H3.

Operators set these fields at H1 when approving a plan; afterward the conductor and S0 scripts maintain them through dual-write to journal and state.json. If identity drifts (duplicate ids, wrong parent), pursuit turns attach evidence to the wrong scope and goal_verify cannot succeed. See [Vision §3 — Branch A](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#3-branch-a-pursuit-control-plane) for how the goal model anchors Plane A.
